identity.name: Lockport Energy Associates LP
identity.regulatory_caveat: NR = Non-Regulated, merchant. No FERC Form 1 coverage. Financial data limited; LTSA terms pending data room extraction (see ltsa_terms.yaml).

cogen.source: engineering.yaml plant.is_chp = true; EIA-860 schedule 3_1 'Associated with CHP System = Yes' for all 4 generators
cogen.evidence: DHTS (Daily Heat Tape Schedule) infrastructure present per renewablesinfo equipment profile; HRSGs configured with steam-export capability; PURPA-era 1992 plant designed for cogeneration as primary purpose; Entity Type Q (cogeneration entity) per EIA-860; MOR data shows winter steam-driven dispatch patterns. STEAM HOST identified (2026-05-26 web audit): the original cogen host is the Harrison Radiator Division of General Motors (steam + electricity delivered to the GM Harrison Radiator plant in Lockport, NY); electricity also sold to NYSEG (New York State Electric & Gas) under a PURPA-era arrangement.

mid_merit.source: Derived from engineering.yaml: 3×1 CCGT configuration, F-class, 1992 vintage; ramp adequate; HR mix (8,901-10,424 Btu/kWh across modes per operating_profile.yaml) is competitive in mid-merit dispatch
mid_merit.evidence: Cycle-tolerant 3×1 CCGT supports daily on/off and intra-day ramping. Heat rate range from operating_profile.yaml is in mid-merit-economic range. MOR data shows historical mid-merit-realized behavior during summer months.

baseload.source: Derived from engineering.yaml: F-class CCGT, continuous-duty design; HR efficient enough for sustained operation; no structural barrier to high-CF operation
baseload.evidence: Hardware supports continuous duty. Baseload capability is structurally present in the envelope even though current realization is well below baseload CF (~12-15% observed vs >70% baseload threshold).

peaker.source: EIA-860 3_1 time_to_full_load_min = 720 (12 hr) for all 4 generators, via renewablesinfo_org thermal_enriched.parquet (queried 2026-05-26)
peaker.evidence: Cold-start-to-full-load is 720 min (12 hr) per EIA — far slower than fast-response peaker duty (which needs minutes). Frame-class CCGT (CT prime movers + CA steam side), not aeroderivative. Even setting aside formal certification, the 12-hr start physically disqualifies fast peaker duty.

frequency_regulation.source: NYISO AGC qualification status unverified. Web audit 2026-05-26 finding: per-plant AGC/regulation participation is NOT publicly retrievable (NYISO does not publish a public AGC-qualified-resource list at plant grain). Does not yield to a D1 web check.
frequency_regulation.evidence: Hardware *may* partly suffice (the CA ramps; CTs have a high 62% floor), but AGC qualification is a binary formal certification, not a hardware property — and it's not publicly disclosed per-plant. Genuinely unknown; needs D2 or operator inquiry.

must_run_eligible.source: Composite: at least one mechanism (steam_host_obligation) is structurally present and active per MOR data. See mechanism sub-blocks for the breakdown.
must_run_eligible.evidence: Multiple must-run mechanisms may apply to Lockport. Most-confidently active: steam-host obligation during DHTS-driven periods. Likely also: PURPA QF status (1992 PURPA-era + Entity Type Q + IPP CHP sector all consistent with QF). Not active: RMR (Lockport not on NYISO RMR list). Open: NYISO ICAP capacity-firm participation.

realized_operating_profile.trend_flag: Generation trending UP: annual CF 2.9% (2021) -> 21.0% (2025), ~2x prior years. Possible realization shift (market / merit-order change) worth tracking — the kind of transition the realized-profile concept is meant to surface.
realized_operating_profile.trend_interpretation: Steam delivery is stable year-over-year (cogen obligation steady); the rising CF is incremental electricity generation. So the realized-profile shift is 'more mid-merit on top of steady cogen', not a cogen change.

plant_profile.regime_note: Structural anchor only — capability envelope + realized profile define what plant is being modeled, but v1 engine output does not condition dispatch on either. Forward conditioning on realized profile is the v2 payoff (ADR-005 §4).

sensitivity_ranks.rationale: Fuel is the largest single cost stream. A 25% move in Henry Hub moves the headline through the dispatch profitability boundary.
sensitivity_ranks.source: Henry Hub historical series; user-adjusted multiplier

sensitivity_ranks.rationale: A enters freshly overhauled (MI taken in history); B/C enter mid-life carrying the deferred MI. Most of the gap is inherited state, not in-forward policy. B≈C in a 1-yr horizon because C's distinctive headroom<1,000 regime is never reached.
Fresh start parks EOH ~20k from MI threshold → wear premium dormant for all policies. Aged start is required for A/B/C to bind. Carries an inspection in/out of window.
sensitivity_ranks.source: ADR-009 § initial-condition; default init_state EOH=24k → MI fires in-horizon

policy.source: Prototype understanding doc §5; relabeled per ADR-009 (A=myopic merchant, B=NPV-rational, C=risk-averse).
policy.why_v1: Bracketing knob, not optimizer. A/B differ via near-threshold wear premium; magnitudes (2.5×/4.0×) deferred for re-derivation.
policy.upgrade: Re-derive premium from inspection pull-forward economics once revenue stack + real LTSA terms are in.

gas_price.source: Henry Hub daily series, sim window 1999-2026 (RT basis).
gas_price.why_v1: Largest single cost driver, directly observed. Multiplier knob brackets sensitivity for showcase.
gas_price.upgrade: Forward-curve anchoring + basis differential (Iroquois/TGP) for delivered cost; locational gas pricing for v2.

init_state.source: Default EOH=24k 'post-HGP mid-clock'; aged option uses ADR-009 mode-specific historical end-state.
init_state.why_v1: Initial EOH is first-order on MI timing. Aged option reveals A/B/C divergence; fresh shows the dormant-policy artifact for transparency.
init_state.upgrade: Calibrate from MOR / data-room (1992 vintage, accrued EOH); flagged in parameter_calibration_register.md §3.12.

scenario.why_v1: Captures multiple gas regimes including high-gas years. Probability-weighted, not equal-weighted.
scenario.upgrade: Joint price/gas/regime conditioning; winter (Nov-Mar) climate-index conditioning; multi-year horizon.

capacity_revenue.source: NYISO ICAP Zone A pricing not yet modeled.
capacity_revenue.why_v1: Cogen survivorship reality is largely capacity + steam revenue. Excluded by design so the energy-only headline cannot be misread as valuation.
capacity_revenue.upgrade: Add ICAP module ($/kW-mo × CSF × CR factor); document MAS auction history. Strategic spine §2.4.

steam_revenue.source: Steam host contract not in data room yet.
steam_revenue.why_v1: Required for cogen valuation but assumption-heavy. Excluded so engine mechanics are not entangled with host-contract assumptions.
steam_revenue.upgrade: Model steam $/Mlb with host take/pay terms; couple HRSG output to capacity envelope.

ltsa_deeper.source: Athens-prototype LTSA terms (fixed fee $850k/mo, EOH reserve $175/EOH, MI $30M/65% OEM, CI $3.75M/75% OEM).
ltsa_deeper.why_v1: Placeholders structurally correct (7 streams), values not contract-grade.
ltsa_deeper.upgrade: Real Lockport LTSA terms from data room (PURPA-era contract + amendments).

multi_year.why_v1: Forward A=B=C overlap is now resolved on 1yr (aged start). Multi-year is where the in-forward policy effect truly bites (C's headroom<1,000 regime becomes reachable).
multi_year.upgrade: Multi-year forward with aged-state hand-off across years; aging-clock anchored to 1992 vintage.

wear_constants.source: Wear physics traces to [GER-3620] (equivalent-hours convention, trip-from-load factored starts), [Kumar2012] (start C&M), and F-class fatigue/TBC literature. Load × ambient creep coupling has analog evidence in [Saturday-Isaiah-2018] (LM2500+ Cranfield-PYTHIA + Larson-Miller + MLRI).
wear_constants.why_v1: Wear physics defensible at literature/vendor level (Tier-2 cited). Per-constant sensitivity rank in parameter_calibration_register §3.
wear_constants.upgrade: Cross-check AMBIENT_WEAR_SENS_PER_F against [Saturday-Isaiah-2018] (6.85%/°F LM2500+ vs current 0.4%/°F — ~17× delta; heavy-duty F-class vs aero-derivative caveat — register §3.7). Then [NERC-GADS] Class CC EFOR calibration for HRSG/BG baselines → Tier-3 calibrated.

GER-3620.title: GE Power, GER-3620: Heavy-Duty Gas Turbine Operating and Maintenance Considerations

Kumar2012.title: Kumar et al., Power Plant Cycling Costs (NREL/SR-5500-55433), 2012 — Table 1-1 Gas-CC start C&M

NERC-GADS.title: NERC Generating Availability Data System (GADS) — Class CC availability statistics

Saturday-Isaiah-2018.title: Saturday & Isaiah (2018), Effects of Ambient Temperature and Shaft Power Variations on Creep Life Consumption of Industrial Gas Turbine Blades, Energy and Power Engineering 10, 120-131
